description_long: A scattered cluster of irregularly shaped stones, each with a rough, weathered surface and natural earth-toned coloration. The stones vary in size, with some larger, rounded rocks and several smaller, flatter pieces interspersed among them. Their surfaces display subtle mottling and textural variations, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. The arrangement appears random, as if the stones have naturally accumulated or been loosely placed together. The coloration ranges from light tan to muted gray-brown, with occasional hints of mossy green. These stones would be commonly found in urban parks, along pathways, or as decorative barriers in landscaped areas.
description: A group of irregular, earth-toned stones with rough, weathered surfaces, scattered in a natural arrangement. The stones vary in size and shape, displaying mottled textures and subtle color variations.
description_view_0: From this angle, the stones appear loosely clustered, with several larger rocks in the foreground and smaller stones scattered behind.
description_view_1: This view highlights the uneven distribution, with more stones visible on the left and a few isolated pieces on the right.
description_view_2: The stones are seen from a slightly elevated perspective, emphasizing their varying heights and the random gaps between them.
description_view_3: Here, the stones are more spread out, with the largest rocks positioned toward the center and smaller stones forming a loose perimeter.
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and natural textural variations, with no regular or artificial patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with minor surface pitting and slight discoloration from exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or landscaped greenery, often partially embedded or surrounded by grass or mulch.
